 {"id":"bd-r24e","title":"Gate-aware beads-release.formula v2","description":"Rewrite beads-release.formula.toml with gates for async waits.

## Design
See ultrathink analysis in bd-d1n1 epic.

## Key Changes
1. Split into phases: Prep, Gate, Verify, Install
2. await-ci step with gate.type=gh:run
3. Parallel verification after gate (verify-github, verify-npm, verify-pypi)
4. Fan-in at local-install

## Formula Structure
```toml
# Phase 1: Prep & Push
[[steps]]
id = "push-tag"
...

# Gate: CI Completion  
[[steps]]
id = "await-ci"
needs = ["push-tag"]
[steps.gate]
type = "gh:run"
workflow = "release.yml"
timeout = "30m"

# Phase 2: Verify (parallel)
[[steps]]
id = "verify-github"
needs = ["await-ci"]
...
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Formula uses gate syntax
- [ ] Phases cleanly separated
- [ ] Parallel verification works
- [ ] Successfully releases beads","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-02T13:27:42.414518-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:22:07.770475-08:00","closed_at":"2026-01-02T16:52:15.309685-08:00","close_reason":"Formula v2 complete with gate syntax, phases, and parallel verification","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-r24e","depends_on_id":"bd-d1n1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-02T13:28:18.643128-08:00","created_by":"mayor"},{"issue_id":"bd-r24e","depends_on_id":"bd-quw1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-02T13:28:18.711394-08:00","created_by":"mayor"},{"issue_id":"bd-r24e","depends_on_id":"bd-z6kw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-02T13:28:18.732758-08:00","created_by":"mayor"}]}
 {"id":"bd-r2n1","title":"Add integration tests for RPC server and event loops","description":"After adding basic unit tests for daemon utilities, the complex daemon functions still need integration tests:

Core daemon lifecycle:
- startRPCServer: Initializes and starts RPC server with proper error handling
- runEventLoop: Polling-based sync loop with parent monitoring and signal handling
- runDaemonLoop: Main daemon initialization and setup

Health checking:
- isDaemonHealthy: Checks daemon responsiveness and health metrics
- checkDaemonHealth: Periodic health verification

These require more complex test infrastructure (mock RPC, test contexts, signal handling) and should be tackled after the unit test foundation is in place.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T12:28:56.022996362-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T12:44:32.167862713-07:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T12:44:32.167862713-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-r2n1","depends_on_id":"bd-4or","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-18T12:28:56.045893852-07:00","created_by":"mhwilkie"}]}

 {"id":"bd-r4od","title":"Parameterize SQLite busy_timeout in store.go","description":"Modify sqlite.New() to accept configurable busy_timeout.

## Current State
`internal/storage/sqlite/store.go` hardcodes:
```go
connStr = "file:" + path + "?_pragma=foreign_keys(ON)&_pragma=busy_timeout(30000)&..."
```

## Implementation Options

### Option A: Add timeout parameter to New()
```go
func New(ctx context.Context, path string, busyTimeout time.Duration) (*SQLiteStorage, error)
```
Pro: Simple, explicit
Con: Changes function signature (breaking change for callers)

### Option B: Options struct pattern
```go
type Options struct {
    BusyTimeout time.Duration // default 30s
}
func New(ctx context.Context, path string, opts *Options) (*SQLiteStorage, error)
```
Pro: Extensible, nil means defaults
Con: More boilerplate

### Recommendation: Option A with default
```go
func New(ctx context.Context, path string) (*SQLiteStorage, error) {
    return NewWithOptions(ctx, path, 30*time.Second)
}

func NewWithOptions(ctx context.Context, path string, busyTimeout time.Duration) (*SQLiteStorage, error)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- busy_timeout is parameterized in connection string
- 0 timeout means no waiting (immediate SQLITE_BUSY)
- All existing callers continue to work (via default wrapper)

 {"id":"bd-r4sn","title":"Phase 2.5: TOON-based daemon sync","description":"Implement TOON-native daemon sync (replaces JSONL sync machinery).

## Overview
Daemon sync is the final integration point. Replace export/import/merge machinery with TOON-native sync, building on deletion tracking (2.3) and merge optimization (2.4).

## Required Work

### 2.5.1 TOON-based Daemon Sync
- [ ] Understand current JSONL sync machinery (export.go, import.go, merge.go)
- [ ] Replace export step with TOON encoding (EncodeTOON)
- [ ] Replace import step with TOON decoding (DecodeTOON)
- [ ] Replace merge step with TOON-aware 3-way merge
- [ ] Update daemon auto-sync to read/write TOON
- [ ] Verify 5-second debounce still works

### 2.5.2 Deletion Sync Integration
- [ ] Load deletions.toon during import phase
- [ ] Apply deletions after merging issues
- [ ] Ensure deletion TTL respects daemon schedule

### 2.5.3 Testing
- [ ] Unit tests for daemon sync with TOON
- [ ] Integration tests with actual daemon operations
- [ ] Multi-clone sync scenarios with concurrent edits
- [ ] Performance comparison with JSONL sync
- [ ] Long-running daemon stability tests

## Success Criteria
- Daemon reads/writes TOON format (not JSONL)
- Sync latency comparable to JSONL (<100ms)
- All 70+ tests passing
- bdt commands work seamlessly with daemon
- Multi-clone sync scenarios work correctly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:20.33132177-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:42:25.274362-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:42:25.274362-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-r4sn","depends_on_id":"bd-uz8r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:20.347724699-07:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-r4sn","depends_on_id":"bd-uwkp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:43:20.355379309-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
